topic: Time - Duration Arithmetic and Checked Operations

turns.content: I'm trying to calculate a timeout duration in my config struct, but I keep hitting an overflow panic when multiplying seconds by u32. Should I just use saturating_add instead of checked operations?

Saturating will clamp values at the maximum limit rather than letting you know if your calculation is wrong, which might hide bugs in your config logic. You should probably stick with Duration::from_secs or from_millis for building base durations and then use checked_add to safely combine them without losing information about overflows.
That makes sense, but what if I need to subtract two durations that are close together? Will Duration::sub panic silently or give me an error?

Duration::sub will panic with a message telling you exactly where the overflow occurred so you can fix your logic immediately. If you want to avoid panics entirely, check if both durations are positive first or use saturating_sub which returns Duration::ZERO on underflow instead of crashing.
